---
title: "rician_noise"
generated: true
description: "Add Rician noise -- the magnitude-image noise model."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.augment.intensity`](../nitrix-augment-intensity.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:rician_noise -->
```python
rician_noise(x: Float[Array, '...'], key: Array, *, sigma: Float[Array, '...']) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:rician_noise -->
Add Rician noise -- the magnitude-image noise model.

Returns $\sqrt{(x + n_r)^2 + n_i^2}$ with independent noise
terms $n_r, n_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2)$.  Reduces to
$|x|$ at $\sigma = 0$.  This is the noise distribution of
the magnitude of a complex signal whose real and imaginary parts carry
independent Gaussian perturbations.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:rician_noise -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Float` | `...` | — | Intensity tensor, interpreted as the true magnitude signal. |
| `key` | `Array` | — | — | PRNG key. It is split into two independent streams for the real and imaginary noise components. |
| `sigma` | `Float` | `...` | — | Standard deviation of the Gaussian perturbation on each of the real and imaginary parts (scalar or broadcastable to `x`). Drawing it randomly is the caller's concern. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:rician_noise -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
